meta_title: DharmaMind — Building intelligence guided by wisdom
meta_description: DharmaMind brings Sanatana Dharma and artificial intelligence together — dharmic guidance, ethical intelligence research, and technology built on the eternal wisdom tradition.
hero_subtitle: DharmaMind brings together Sanatana Dharma, ethical intelligence research, and artificial intelligence — technology rooted in a tradition, offered to anyone seeking clarity.
why_title: Power without wisdom rarely leads to better decisions.
why_body: Intelligence has grown faster than our capacity for discernment. DharmaMind draws on one of the world's oldest and deepest traditions of ethical reasoning — Sanatana Dharma — to build technology and research that help people act with clarity, not just speed.

pillars.title: Wisdom
pillars.body: The Bhagavad Gita, the Upanishads, and the Yoga Sutras — living philosophical traditions exploring right action, self-knowledge, and responsibility.

pillars.title: Research
pillars.body: Interdisciplinary inquiry into Dharmic Intelligence — a proposed framework for ethical discernment, contextual wisdom, and human flourishing.

pillars.title: Technology
pillars.body: AI products designed to support reflection, right action, and wiser decisions — grounded in real scripture, not generic self-help.

ecosystem.title: DharmaMind AI
ecosystem.body: A guided AI experience for dharmic reflection, personal decisions, and daily practice — free to start, rooted in real scripture, never generic.

ecosystem.title: DharmaMind Research
ecosystem.body: Original interdisciplinary research on Dharmic Intelligence — ethical discernment, contextual wisdom, and their application to human decision-making and AI.

ecosystem.title: DharmaMind Community
ecosystem.body: A future network of researchers, practitioners, and technologists building wisdom-centered work in the open.

di_teaser.label: The Theory
di_teaser.title: Dharmic Intelligence
di_teaser.body: A proposed framework for understanding the capacity to discern and act on what is right — not merely what is clever or convenient — through self-awareness, ethical judgment, compassion, and contextual wisdom. Currently being developed through DharmaMind Research.

footer_bottom: © 2026 DharmaMind. Proudly rooted in Sanatana Dharma, offered to every seeker.
